6PM CBB: VCU vs. Rhode Island

@cappersfree

VCU TTO 74.5 -115 3U (DK)
URI TTO 70.5 -112 3U (FD)
VCU URI 1H Over 69.5 -106 3U (FD)

6PM CBB: VCU vs. Rhode Island

As VCU and Rhode Island get set to meet for the first time this season in intra-Atlantic 10 action, these teams enter playing drastically different styles of basketball. VCU has played at an up-tempo pace, posting an overall record of 10-7 to the over on the season and 3-1 to the over in conference play. On the other side, Rhode Island has taken a far more methodical approach, resulting in an over-under record of 6-11 to the under overall and 1-3 to the under in conference action. Tonight, we should see the superior VCU squad go on the road and get the tempo.

VCU has managed to score 81 points per game in conference play despite shooting just 45% from the field and 30% from three-point range. They now face a Rhode Island defense allowing 46% from the field and 40% from three-point range in conference play. Rhode Island is surrendering just 62 points per game, but they are doing so while allowing 14 field goal attempts and 6 made three-point field goals per game, numbers that are set to rise against a VCU offense averaging 17 field goal attempts and 7.5 made three-point field goals per game in conference play.

On the other side, while Rhode Island has managed to score just 63 points per game in conference action, that output is set for a rise. They have shot only 41% from the field and 30% from three-point range, while attempting just 54 field goal attempts per game. They are, however, taking on a VCU defense allowing 57% from the field, 35% from three-point range, and 58 field goal attempts per game in conference play. VCU is surrendering 80 points per game while allowing 5.8 made three-point field goals per game, facing a Rhode Island offense averaging 8.5 made three-point field goals per game in conference action.

Rhode Island is playing at just the 273rd-slowest pace in the country, dishing out assists on 55.7% of their possessions. VCU is playing at the 152nd-slowest pace, assisting on just 48% of their possessions. VCU is getting 16.8 points per game from the bench while allowing 16.5 points. Rhode Island is getting only 7.8 points per game from the bench while allowing 16.8 points.

VCU: Quentin Hill (illness)
Rhode Island: Ishmael El-Amin (suspension)

7PM CBB: Davidson Vs. George Washington

Through the last seven meetings played between Davidson
and George Washington on the hardwood, these teams have
combined for an over-under record of 5-1-1 favoring the over.
Tonight, as these Atlantic 10 rivals meet for the first time this
season, we should see another tight final score as George
Washington forces Davidson to play up to their pace.

Through four conference games, Davidson has managed to
score 67.2 points per game, shooting just 41% from the field
while attempting only 56 field goal attempts per game. Tonight,
they take on a George Washington defense surrendering 43%
from the field and 58 field goal attempts per game in
conference play. George Washington has allowed 71.2 points
per game in conference action despite holding opponents to
just 30% from three-point range and 7.5 made three-point field
goals per game, numbers that are set to rise against a
Davidson offense hitting 33% from three-point range and 9
made three-point field goals per game in conference play.

On the other side of the ball, Davidson is surrendering 70.0
points per game in conference action, and those numbers are
set to climb. They have allowed 43% from the field, 32% from
three-point range, 56 field goal attempts per game, and 8
made three-point field goals per game thus far. Tonight, they
face a George Washington offense posting 51% from the field,
39% from three-point field, 61 field goal attempts per game,
and 13 made three-point field goals per game in conference
play, translating to 87.2 points per game.

Even while playing at the 332nd-ranked pace of play
nationally (bottom 9%), Davidson has scored 72.3 points per
game and surrendered 69.2 points per game, producing an
average game total of 141.5 points. Tonight, as they go on the
road to face a superior George Washington team playing at the
61st-fastest pace in the country (top 17%), the game total is
set to accelerate significantly, covering a number that
is

ALL PLAYS 1/14/2025

NBA PLAYS

(SU) CAVALIERS ML -105
In a true coin-flip matchup, the value sits with the discounted side — and that’s Cleveland. The Cavs have been a brutal ATS team all season (12-29) while Philly is one of the best (23-14-1), which naturally inflates this line toward the Sixers and creates value on the dog. Cleveland already beat Philly once, has the shooting recipe to repeat it, and they’ve shown they won’t shy away from hostile, playoff-style environments. Simply put, the wrong side is favored. Cavs ML.

NCAAB PLAYS

(SU) VANDERBILT -3.5 -110
Vandy is rolling and Texas can’t guard. The Longhorns are dead last in the SEC in defensive efficiency and have allowed 85+ in every conference game — a brutal matchup against an elite Vandy offense. Texas has also gone 0-4 vs top-20 defenses, losing by double digits.

9PM CBB: South Carolina Vs. Arkansas

Picks:
SC ARK Over 155.5 -125 SU (DK)
SC TTO 71.5 -120 3U (FD)
ARK TTO 83.5 -131 3U (DK)
SC ARK 1H Over 73.5 -122 3U (FD)

9PM CBB: South Carolina Vs. Arkansas

Through the last ten meetings between South Carolina and Arkansas, these teams have split the over-under 5-5. Tonight, however, this matchup sets up very differently as two teams with drastically opposing tempo collide. South Carolina has operated at one of the slowest paces in the country, while Arkansas has consistently pushed tempo every time they have taken the floor. With Arkansas at home and clearly the superior side, their pace is set to dictate the flow of this game.

Through three conference games, South Carolina is scoring 73 points per game while shooting 43% from the field and 35% from three-point range on just 56 field goal attempts per game. Tonight, they face Arkansas defense allowing 53% from the field and 38% from three-point range while giving up 60 field goal attempts per game in conference play. Arkansas has surrendered 86 points per game in conference on 77 made three-point field goals per game, a profile that lines up poorly against a South Carolina offense averaging 9 made three-point field goals per game.

On the other side, South Carolina is surrendering 75.5 points per game in conference action. They have allowed 43% from the field, 32% from three-point range, and 8 made three-point field goals per game. Tonight, they face an Arkansas offense shooting 46% from the field and 37% from three-point range while averaging 8.5 made three-point field goals per game. Arkansas is scoring 85 points per game in conference play on 59 field goal attempts, a matched up against a South Carolina defense allowing 61 field goal attempts per game.

Even while playing at the 307th-ranked pace nationally (bottom 15%), South Carolina is still scoring 80 points per game and surrendering 70 points per game, producing an average game total of 150. Tonight, as they go up and down at a much higher tempo, we expect the scoring to rise. We’re also keying in on South Carolina’s ability to shoot three-pointers at a 35% clip, which is 11% better than their season average.

Texas +5.5 over Vandy is a good read in my opinion. Vandy has played out of their minds this season offensively so far. But this is not as good of a team as the record shows. Texas Tech by 20+ tonight vs Utah. Really just banking on the better team showing up and running them out the gym. Texas Tech is very capable of that

Upgrading Kansas State -1.5 -110 from a 1U play to 3U POTW
@cappersfree
Analysis 📍

UCF comes in hot at 9-1 in their last 10, while Kansas
State sits on a three-game skid, but this is a classic
buy-low spot. K-State’s shooting profile stands out,
ranking top 30 in both threes made and three-point
percentage, and they also have the best player on the
floor in PJ Haggerty, an All-American caliber guard.
Both teams can be sloppy with the ball, but talent
and shot-making tend to win these spots, especially
at home. With UCF’s recent form inflating the line and
their underlying metrics less impressive, I’ll buy low
on Kansas State at the shortest price point.

NBA: PLAY OF THE NIGHT 🔮

3U: Charlotte Hornets +4.5

LIKE IF TALING

#NBA | #NBABets | #SportsBettingX | #CharlotteHornets | #HiveMentality 🔮

8-Unit Play. Take #694 North Carolina (-12) over Notre Dame (7 p.m., Wednesday, Jan. 21)

I think that Notre Dame is a dead stick. Their season went in the tank the minute that Markus Burton went down for the year. Even with Burton, I had my doubts about this as an NCAA tournament team. Without him they are sunk. Micah Shrewsbury may not make it to the end of the year on the Notre Dame bench. He has been an embarrassment. Whether it was calling out the Irish fans last year, attacking the refs after a Cal loss this year, or just being a dick at the end of the Virginia Tech game last Saturday, he looks like a dead man walking. I think he is losing the locker room as well. They have lost four straight and the losses are getting worse, not better, coming by 15, 12 and 13 points. They are facing a North Carolina team that is in a foul mood. The Tar Heels got swept on their West Coast trip last weekend, falling at Cal and Stanford. Now they are at home and they need a win to get things going. Notre Dame has not played well at Chapel Hill at all, losing by 20, 23, 17, and 42 points in their last four trips. They have only lost by fewer than 11 points here once since the end of 2018. I don't think that Notre Dame is going to be able to handle the size and athleticism of the Tar Heels. I also think the home team is going to be able to set the tempo and that means an up and down game. North Carolina hasn't trucked anyone in a while. This feels like a game where they can empty the tanks and run a tired, worn out Notre Dame team off the court.

1-Unit Play. Take #720 Wichita State (-11.5) over East Carolina (7:30 p.m., Wednesday, Jan. 21)

Thomas Casale
